instruction: Inside ~/Desktop/log_audit, collect all unique service names whose log lines contain level ERROR or CRITICAL and status >=500. Ignore anything under archive/. Save the sorted service names (one per line) to ~/Desktop/log_audit/report/failing_services.txt.

parameters.command:
cat > ~/Desktop/log_audit/app1/api.log <<'EOF'
2026-02-01T10:00:00Z service=auth level=INFO status=200 msg=ok
2026-02-01T10:02:00Z service=auth level=ERROR status=503 msg=upstream_timeout
2026-02-01T10:03:00Z service=search level=ERROR status=404 msg=not_found
2026-02-01T10:04:00Z service=billing level=CRITICAL status=500 msg=db_down
EOF

cat > ~/Desktop/log_audit/app2/sub/worker.log <<'EOF'
2026-02-01T11:00:00Z service=cache level=CRITICAL status=502 msg=panic
2026-02-01T11:01:00Z service=auth level=ERROR status=500 msg=retry_failed
2026-02-01T11:02:00Z service=payments level=ERROR status=500 msg=gateway_fail
2026-02-01T11:03:00Z service=analytics level=INFO status=200 msg=ok
EOF

cat > ~/Desktop/log_audit/archive/old.log <<'EOF'
2026-02-01T09:00:00Z service=legacy level=CRITICAL status=503 msg=should_be_ignored
EOF

command: import pyautogui; import time; pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5);
